jobs.subject: CSS default cursor: slim glass arrow pointer with navy core, teal rim light, tiny gold tip highlight. Classic idle-navigation arrow; hotspot at arrow tip (top-left). Minimal detail.
CSS pointer cursor: minimalist glass pointing hand OR chevron-hand hybrid — index extended, emerald/teal luminous edge, gold knuckle accent. One hand only, clearly clickable, not cartoon.
CSS grab cursor: open glass grip brackets or half-closed tactical hand ready to drag a slider. Emerald edge glow, navy glass fill, gold hinge accent.
CSS grabbing cursor: closed glass grip / clenched tactical hand actively dragging. Same glass material language as grab state but clearly closed.
CSS wait cursor: tiny glass hourglass or teal spinning wait ring. Blocking busy state, no digits.
CSS context-menu cursor: glass arrow pointer plus a small stacked glass menu tile beside it. Teal borders, navy fill, no text lines that read as letters.
CSS help cursor: glossy glass coin-badge with a single teal question-mark symbol. Token-logo buckle language, no other text.
CSS progress cursor: glass default arrow plus a small partial teal progress ring. Working but still interactive; extremely simple.
CSS text cursor: tall glass I-beam caret with teal stem, gold cap highlights, navy outline. Very narrow, no letters.
CSS not-allowed cursor: glass prohibition disc with warm gold rim and coral-red diagonal slash. Friendly but clear; no words.
